name: fda-database
description: Query openFDA API for drugs, devices, adverse events, recalls, regulatory submissions (510k, PMA), substance identification (UNII), for FDA regulatory data analysis and safety research.
capabilities: Query drug adverse events and labeling information from FDA databases
Search medical device recalls, 510k clearances, and adverse events
Look up food recalls and dietary supplement safety data
Access substance information including UNII codes and CAS numbers
Monitor veterinary drug adverse events by species and breed
Compare safety profiles across multiple drugs or devices
author: K-Dense-AI
securitySummary: This is a legitimate API wrapper for the FDA's public openFDA API (api.fda.gov). All static findings are false positives: the 'CRITICAL' heuristic flagged code execution + network + credential access as suspicious, but this is standard API client behavior for authenticating with a legitimate government API. MD5 is used for cache key generation, not cryptographic security. Documentation code blocks were misidentified as shell commands. All network requests go to the official FDA API endpoint with no exfiltration or credential theft patterns.
valueStatement: Access comprehensive FDA regulatory data including drug adverse events, medical device recalls, and safety information through the official openFDA API. Build pharmacovigilance and regulatory research workflows with Python.
userTitle: Query FDA drug and device databases
limitations: API rate limits apply (240 requests per minute, 1000 per day without key)
Only queries public FDA data; no access to confidential internal records
Historical data availability varies by endpoint and data category

useCases.title: Drug safety monitoring
useCases.description: Monitor adverse event reports and identify safety signals for medications

useCases.title: Device surveillance
useCases.description: Track medical device adverse events and recall status for products

useCases.title: Regulatory compliance
useCases.description: Research approval pathways and compliance history for submissions

promptTemplates.title: Basic drug lookup
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the FDA database skill to look up adverse events for aspirin and show the 5 most common reactions

promptTemplates.title: Device recall search
promptTemplates.prompt: Query the FDA database for medical device recalls involving pacemakers in the past year

promptTemplates.title: Comparative analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Compare adverse event rates between ibuprofen, naproxen, and aspirin using the FDA database

promptTemplates.title: Food safety monitoring
promptTemplates.prompt: Monitor food recalls related to undeclared peanuts and show the most recent 10 recalls

bestPractices: Set the FDA_API_KEY environment variable to increase rate limits from 1,000 to 120,000 requests per day
Use specific search terms and field queries for accurate results rather than broad wildcards
Implement caching for frequently accessed data to reduce API calls and improve performance
antiPatterns: Do not use overly broad wildcard searches that return excessive unrelated results
Avoid making rapid successive requests without rate limiting, which may trigger 429 errors
Do not assume all records contain all fields; always check field existence before accessing

faq.question: Is an API key required?
faq.answer: No, the FDA API works without a key but has lower rate limits (1,000/day vs 120,000/day with a key).

faq.question: What data categories are available?
faq.answer: Drugs, devices, foods, animal/veterinary products, and chemical substances are supported.

faq.question: How current is the data?
faq.answer: Data is updated regularly but may have a lag of days to weeks depending on the endpoint.

faq.question: Can I download full datasets?
faq.answer: The API returns paginated results. For bulk data, consider downloading official FDA data files separately.

faq.question: What are the rate limits?
faq.answer: 240 requests per minute with or without a key. Daily limits are 1,000 without a key and 120,000 with a key.

faq.question: Is this data suitable for clinical decisions?
faq.answer: This is public post-market surveillance data. Use official prescribing information for clinical decisions.
